_general.purpose: Trained-model inference. The `inference` action dispatches through the trained_model registry (see work.trained_model_list) and loads the live ONNX session from go/internal/ml/. Per-task convenience actions (route_query, curation_score, forge_suggest_surfaces, …) register on top of this surface when their model is promoted.
_general.notes:
SURFACE BOUNDARY. ml inference happens here; trained_model lifecycle (forge / list / promote / retire) lives on the work surface (work.trained_model_*). Read-side telemetry (model_predictions side-table) is written by this surface but queried via projections that future eval dashboards consume.

MODEL RESOLUTION. ml.inference accepts either `model_id` (specific trained_models.id — useful for the A/B harness when comparing exact versions) or `task` (resolves the currently-promoted row for that task). model_id wins when both supplied. The status='promoted' filter is the only loadable lookup path through `task`; `evaluating` / `ab_testing` rows reach the serving layer only via model_id.

TELEMETRY WRITE. Every successful inference writes one row to model_predictions, content-keyed by features_hash (SHA-256 of canonical-serialized features) and spanned via span_id from the agent-first-substrate envelope. Drift detection + prediction caching + future proj_model_eval projections all consume this table.

LATENCY BUDGET. Inferences are CPU-only via onnxruntime_go; target <50ms per call for the five ml-temp candidate models (source router, curation classifier, cross-encoder reranker, bug surface tagger, skill auto-loader). Latency rows beyond 100ms are warning-shape and surface in future model-eval dashboards.

DEGRADED MODE. When ML_MODELS_ROOT isn't configured or libonnxruntime.so isn't loadable (scripts/setup-ml-deps.sh hasn't been run on this box), the inference action returns a typed error envelope rather than panicking. Convenience actions follow the same pattern.

inference.purpose: Run inference against a trained_model. Accepts either model_id (specific version) or task (resolves the currently-promoted row). Writes a model_predictions telemetry row keyed by span_id + features_hash.

params.name: model_id
params.description: trained_models.id — serve this exact version (useful for the A/B harness when comparing exact versions). Wins when both model_id and task are supplied.

params.name: task
params.description: Task identifier (kebab-case); resolves to the trained_models row where status='promoted'. Resolution is project-scoped, so a task lookup requires project at the dispatch envelope.

params.name: features_data
params.description: Flattened input tensor ([]float32), row-major. len(features_data) must equal the product of features_shape.

params.name: features_shape
params.description: Tensor shape ([]int64) declaring the dims of features_data.

params.name: grounding_event_id
params.description: grounding_events.id when this inference resolves a search-triggered call (cross-encoder reranker, source router); populates model_predictions.grounding_event_id for downstream join-against-clicks projections.

errors.message: ml.inference requires either model_id or task

errors.condition: task supplied without project at the dispatch envelope
errors.message: ml.inference requires project when resolving by task; pass project at the dispatch envelope

errors.condition: no promoted row for the task, or no row with the supplied model_id
the resolved row is in a gated lifecycle state (training / retired)
errors.message: ml: trained model is in a gated lifecycle state (training/retired) (ErrModelGated)

errors.condition: features_data length does not equal the product of features_shape (or either is empty, or a dim is non-positive)
errors.message: ml: input features don't match expected shape (ErrInputShape)

inference.notes:
MODEL RESOLUTION: model_id wins when both are supplied. The status='promoted' filter is the only loadable lookup through `task`; `evaluating` / `ab_testing` rows reach the serving layer only via model_id.

TELEMETRY: every successful inference writes one model_predictions row, content-keyed by features_hash (SHA-256 of canonical-serialized features) and spanned via span_id from the agent-first-substrate envelope.

DEGRADED MODE: when ML_MODELS_ROOT isn't configured or libonnxruntime.so isn't loadable (scripts/setup-ml-deps.sh not run on this box), inference returns a typed error envelope rather than panicking.

SURFACE BOUNDARY: ml inference happens here; trained_model lifecycle (forge / list / promote / retire) lives on the work surface (work.trained_model_*).

returns.description: On success: prediction.output ([]float32) + prediction.output_shape ([]int64); prediction.latency_ms; prediction.model_id (the trained_model.id served); prediction.feat_hash (SHA-256 of canonical-serialized features); span_id (the agent-first-substrate span the model_predictions row was written under); prediction_row_id (model_predictions.id). Caller-controlled errors return as a JSON envelope (error + hint), not a Go error.
